original_hypothesis.title: Unprivileged users can grief/corrupt cost-basis accounting by transferring shares from a zero-balance address (basis not moved) via ERC20Minimal.mint/burn
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: HypoVault overrides transfer/transferFrom to move userBasis proportionally, but it does not override mint/burn flows that may exist in ERC20Minimal. If ERC20Minimal exposes external/public mint/burn (or any mechanism that moves balances without calling HypoVault.transfer/transferFrom), an attacker can cause shares to appear in an account without receiving corresponding basis (or vice versa), enabling performance-fee avoidance or unfair fee extraction on subsequent withdrawals.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Phase A focuses on basis conservation as a critical economic invariant. The vault only updates basis on transfer/transferFrom and request/execute/cancel withdrawal flows. Any alternative balance mutation path breaks INV-ECON-001 and can be exploited by normal users with on-chain actions only.
deployment_assumptions: ERC20Minimal (Panoptic v1.1) exposes external/public mint/burn or another externally reachable method that changes balances without using HypoVault.transfer/transferFrom
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: ERC20Minimal source not included in provided context; must inspect vendored dependency in lib/panoptic-v1.1.

inventory_refs.function: transferFrom(address from, address to, uint256 amount)

proof_of_signal.pattern: Basis is only adjusted inside transfer/transferFrom via _transferBasis; no other hooks for balance mutations

preconditions: There exists at least one externally reachable path that changes share balances without calling HypoVault.transfer/transferFrom (e.g., ERC20Minimal.mint/burn or other)
Attacker can invoke that path to obtain shares with zero/incorrect basis
Withdrawals eventually get fulfilled so attacker can executeWithdrawal and realize fee advantage

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Basis tracking is decoupled from balance changes except in transfer/transferFrom and withdrawal queueing; dependency risk on inherited ERC20Minimal behavior
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: None if ERC20Minimal exposes user-callable balance mutation; otherwise requires privileged actor to mint
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Anytime after attacker can create a basis/balance mismatch

operational_questions: Does ERC20Minimal expose mint/burn externally or via approvals/allowances that bypass overridden functions?
Do any inherited functions mutate balanceOf directly (not via transfer/transferFrom) in a way users can call?
Are there integrations (bridges/wrappers) that call internal mint/burn through delegatecall or hooks?
validation_requirements: Review lib/panoptic-v1.1/contracts/tokens/ERC20Minimal.sol for external/public mint/burn or nonstandard transfer paths
Property test: ensure any balance change implies proportional basis change (or explicit basis initialization) for all reachable paths

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify user-callable function that mints/transfers shares without invoking HypoVault.transfer/transferFrom
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Request withdrawal and later executeWithdrawal to realize reduced performance fees (or cause others to pay more)

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Performance fee mechanism becomes unreliable; accounting disputes likely
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High if basis mismatches are widespread; may require migration
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: withdrawal fee computation and fairness across users

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Normal operations; requires fulfilled withdrawal epochs

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Accounts with shares but unexpectedly low userBasis; sudden basis discontinuities
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Abnormally low aggregate fee revenue vs vault PnL
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Repeated mint/withdraw cycles tied to same accounts

validation_result.impact_summary: No externally reachable mint/burn path exists in HypoVault or standard Panoptic ERC20Minimal; all balance mutations are properly guarded or basis-tracked.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis posits that ERC20Minimal exposes external/public mint/burn functions that bypass HypoVault's transfer/transferFrom overrides (and thus the _transferBasis hook). Analysis of HypoVault.sol shows that:

1. _mintVirtual (line ~410) and _burnVirtual (line ~422) are declared `internal`, making them unreachable by external callers.
2. The only externally reachable balance-mutation functions inherited from ERC20Minimal are transfer() and transferFrom(), both of which are properly overridden in HypoVault (lines 219-248) to include _transferBasis.
3. HypoVault itself controls all other balance-changing paths: executeDeposit calls _mintVirtual and sets userBasis, requestWithdrawal calls _burnVirtual and moves basis to PendingWithdrawal, cancelWithdrawal restores via _mintVirtual and returns basis.
4. Standard Panoptic ERC20Minimal (well-known open-source contract) exposes only transfer, transferFrom, and approve as external/public functions—no public mint or burn.
5. The manage() function could theoretically call the vault itself, but it requires onlyManager access—not available to unprivileged users.
6. Multicall uses delegatecall which preserves msg.sender context, but there is no public mint/burn function to batch through it.

The critical precondition—'There exists at least one externally reachable path that changes share balances without calling HypoVault.transfer/transferFrom'—is not satisfied by the code in scope. All balance mutations for unprivileged users go through properly basis-tracked paths.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: HypoVault.sol lines 406-427 show _mintVirtual and _burnVirtual are both declared `internal`. Lines 219-248 show transfer and transferFrom are properly overridden with _transferBasis. Lines 250-264 show _transferBasis correctly moves basis proportionally. No public/external mint or burn function exists in HypoVault. The ERC20Minimal source is not in scope but is a known minimal implementation without public mint/burn.

execution_path_analysis.entry_point: N/A - no vulnerable entry point exists
calling_sequence: Hypothesis requires: external user -> ERC20Minimal.mint/burn (public) -> balanceOf change without basis update
transfer() overridden at HypoVault.sol:219 with _transferBasis call
transferFrom() overridden at HypoVault.sol:231 with _transferBasis call
No external/public mint or burn function exists in HypoVault
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The hypothesized path does not exist. All external balance-mutation paths in HypoVault either (a) go through overridden transfer/transferFrom with basis tracking, or (b) go through internal-only _mintVirtual/_burnVirtual which are called only from executeDeposit, requestWithdrawal, and cancelWithdrawal—all of which properly update userBasis.

deployment_assumptions_checked: ERC20Minimal (Panoptic v1.1) exposes external/public mint/burn or another externally reachable method that changes balances without using HypoVault.transfer/transferFrom
config_validation.deployment_evidence: ERC20Minimal source is not provided in scope. However, Panoptic v1.1 ERC20Minimal is a well-known open-source contract that only exposes transfer, transferFrom, and approve as external/public functions. No public mint or burn exists in the standard implementation. The HypoVault code itself does not expose any public mint/burn functions—both _mintVirtual and _burnVirtual are declared internal.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario requires an externally callable function that modifies balanceOf without going through transfer/transferFrom. No such function exists in HypoVault (internal-only _mintVirtual/_burnVirtual), and standard Panoptic ERC20Minimal does not expose public mint/burn. The approve() function only modifies allowances, not balances. Even through Multicall, no public mint/burn is available to batch. The manage() function requires onlyManager access. Therefore, no unprivileged user can create a basis/balance mismatch through any available external entry point.
validation_result.impact_assessment: No impact. The hypothesized vulnerability path does not exist because the precondition (externally reachable mint/burn) is not satisfied by the code in scope. The basis tracking system correctly covers all externally accessible balance mutation paths.
recommended_mitigations: Verify that the vendored ERC20Minimal at lib/panoptic-v1.1/contracts/tokens/ERC20Minimal.sol does not expose any public/external mint or burn functions (defensive verification of the assumption)
Consider adding a comment or NatSpec noting that _mintVirtual/_burnVirtual must remain internal to preserve basis invariant
Add a property test asserting that sum(userBasis) + sum(queuedWithdrawal.basis) remains conserved across all external entry points
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Inspect lib/panoptic-v1.1/contracts/tokens/ERC20Minimal.sol for any public/external functions that modify balanceOf or totalSupply. 2. Enumerate all external entry points of HypoVault (including inherited) and verify none reach _mintVirtual/_burnVirtual except through proper basis-tracking paths. 3. Write a Foundry fuzz test calling all external functions as an unprivileged user and asserting basis conservation invariant holds.
uncertainties: ERC20Minimal source code is not provided in scope—validation relies on known behavior of Panoptic v1.1 ERC20Minimal
If a custom/modified ERC20Minimal were used with public mint/burn, the hypothesis would be confirmed
Future modifications to ERC20Minimal or HypoVault could introduce the hypothesized path
additional_testing_needed: Inspect vendored ERC20Minimal source to confirm no public mint/burn
Verify Multicall implementation cannot be used to reach internal functions via delegatecall tricks
Property test: for all external calls, assert sum(userBasis[all]) + sum(queuedWithdrawal.basis[all]) is conserved
